Now, this gets very technical and nerdy pretty quick, but Fleet uses osquery.
OSquery is a way of getting data from operating systems that data is expressed as a SQL table.
So we have documentation for every one of those SQL tables that can express data from enrolled devices.
What he had me do last year was go through the 335 tables that we have for osquery and start making a post about each one.
Now, there were two aspects to that project.
One of them was to make the example queries better, because some of the tables that we have in this documentation don't even have an example.
So that was one part of the project.
The other part of the project, though, was just to say like, hey, what does this table do?
